content: We can frame Self-Refine's iterative un-supervised refinement pipeline as a basic 'skeleton' that can be upgraded by integrating multiple inconsistent self-perspectives from modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a new hierarchical self-refinement framework that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no extra training or supervision. This framework lets the LLM first pull inconsistent critical perspectives from its own parameter space via self-reflection mechanics, then use these perspectives as targeted feedback for each iteration of Self-Refine's refinement loop, rather than relying on vague generic self-feedback.
